insight: Shadowfang's supply is 10 (not 4) because at 4 supply it would not be a credible threat in big-red strategies; opponents could simply ignore it and accept only 4 Fangs, removing the pressure that makes big-red viable. The high supply forces opponents to respond.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about why Shadowfang has 10 supply versus Cynestra's 4 supply.

insight: Cynestra has 4 supply because all units that deal 3 permanent damage share a 4-supply design pattern.
author: kristian1103
context: Discussion comparing supply counts across attacker units.

insight: Bombarder's first few purchases create such a large tempo swing that opponents are forced to wall up, reducing their pressure enough that the Bombarder player can continue purchasing more, creating a self-reinforcing cycle that is hard to break out of.
author: amalloy
context: Community discussion about whether Bombarder needs a nerf and what the specific problem is.

insight: Centurion was considered overpowered at its then-current cost, with masn6811 calling for at least a 2-gold nerf and kristian1103 suggesting it could absorb a 3-gold nerf and still be a great unit.
author: consensus
context: Balance discussion around Bombarder nerf proposals that broadened to other units needing adjustment.

insight: Centurion mirrors allow for a high degree of autopilot play because the unit grants all tech colors and generates strong economy, reducing the need to make key early decisions about tech base composition; this makes it less punishing to play sub-optimally compared to strategies like small-red mirrors.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about Centurion balance and the strategic experience of playing Centurion mirrors.

insight: Deciding on your tech base (e.g., whether to buy an Animus for Tarsier access versus investing in economy) is one of the more skill-testing decisions in Prismata; Centurion reduces this decision point by granting all tech colors automatically.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 explaining why Centurion autopilot is less punishing than other strategies.

insight: In the 'All Gold Costs' event format, Blue is valued at 3 gold while Red and Green are valued at 2 gold each, because a Blastforge produces 1 Blue per turn for 5 gold (1.66 drones), whereas an Animus produces 2 Red for 6 gold (1 drone each) and a Conduit produces 1 Green for 4 gold (1.33 drones), making Blue the most gold-efficient tech resource.
author: siepu
context: Player question about why Blue is priced higher than Red and Green in the All Gold Costs event.

insight: Thorium Dynamo originally gave 4 gold on click in an earlier version of the game.
author: apooche
context: Casual historical note dropped during balance discussion.

insight: masn6811 expressed that balancing units purely for mirror matchups, while producing symmetric games, reduces the interesting macro decisions (such as non-mirror responses) and can make gameplay less fun even if technically balanced; Centurion was cited as the clearest example of this tradeoff.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about Bombarder nerf and the philosophy of balance around mirrors versus non-mirror options.

insight: A community member flagged that the Prismata bot had become out of date, implying it no longer reflects current game balance or strategies.
author: apooche
context: apooche notifying a developer (Wiwiweb) that the bot is outdated.
